# HED-web developer instructions

> **Local environment**: Check `.status/local-environment.md` (not version-controlled) for platform-specific setup — shell, venv activation path, and summary-file conventions.

## Repository overview

Flask-based web application and REST API for HED (Hierarchical Event Descriptors) tools. Python 3.10+, ~20 source files in `hedweb/`, ~10 unit test files in `tests/`, integration tests in `service_tests/`. Deployed via Docker.

**Stack**: Flask, Flask-WTF, hedtools (HED processing library), Jinja2 + Bootstrap frontend, gunicorn in production.

## Project layout

```
hedweb/
  app_factory.py        Flask application factory
  routes.py             All URL route definitions (add new routes here)
  process_form.py       Web form request handling
  process_service.py    REST API request handling
  base_operations.py    Shared logic across operation types
  *_operations.py       Per-domain logic: events, schema, sidecar, spreadsheet, string
  web_util.py           Shared utilities
  constants/            base_constants.py, file_constants.py, page_constants.py, route_constants.py
  templates/            Jinja2 HTML templates
  static/               CSS, JS, images
tests/                  Unit tests (~10 files) — unittest framework
service_tests/          Integration tests — require a running server
deploy/                 Dockerfile, gunicorn config, deploy.sh
pyproject.toml          Build config, dependencies, ruff config (line-length=120, target py310)
config_template.py      Template for config.py — copy and fill in; config.py is gitignored
```

`hedweb/_version.py` is auto-generated by setuptools-scm from git tags; do not edit it manually.

## Environment setup (always do first)

```bash
python -m venv .venv
# activate per your platform (see .status/local-environment.md)
pip install -e .[dev]
```

## Validated commands

```bash
# Run unit tests (unittest, NOT pytest)
python -m unittest discover -s tests -p "test*.py" -v

# Run service/integration tests (requires server running on localhost:5000)
python service_tests/run_service_tests.py --verbose

# Lint
ruff check .

# Auto-fix lint issues
ruff check . --fix

# Format
ruff format .

# Run server locally
python hedweb/runserver.py

# Docker
docker build -t hedweb -f deploy/Dockerfile .
docker run -p 5000:5000 hedweb
```

**Important**: `config.py` must exist before running the server. Copy `config_template.py` to `config.py` and fill in values. `config.py` is gitignored — never commit it.

## Code style

- PEP 8, 120-char line length (configured in `pyproject.toml` `[tool.ruff]`)
- ruff rules: E, W, F, N, B, C4 — run `ruff check .` before committing
- Google-style docstrings for public APIs
- Add type hints to new code where they improve clarity

## CI/CD

GitHub Actions workflows in `.github/workflows/`:
- `ci.yaml` — runs on every push/PR; installs `.[dev]`, runs unit tests (`tests/`) and service tests (`service_tests/`)
- `ruff.yaml` — linting with ruff on every push/PR
- `typos.yaml` — spell-checking
- `mdformat.yaml` — Markdown formatting check
- `docs.yaml` — Sphinx documentation build
- `links.yaml` — link checking
- `claude-code-review.yml` — Claude Code Review on PRs (same-repo and forks via `pull_request_target`); skips drafts and bots
- `claude.yml` — Claude Code assistant; responds to `@claude` mentions in issues/PR comments (restricted to OWNER/MEMBER/COLLABORATOR)

## Commit and PR conventions

- Atomic commits, subject line ≤50 chars, no emojis, no AI attribution
- PRs target `main` branch
- Subject lines are imperative mood, sentence case

## Key conventions

- Routes defined in `hedweb/routes.py`; all business logic goes in `*_operations.py` modules
- Never bypass Flask-WTF CSRF protection in production code
- REST API returns JSON with consistent structure; use HTTP status codes 200/400/422/500
- Unit tests use the real Flask test client; mock only external HED schema network calls
- Do not expose internal hedtools exceptions to end users — wrap them in user-friendly messages
- Do not store secrets in version control; use environment variables or `config.py`

## HED schema notes

- Schema files are XML, named `HED{major}.{minor}.{patch}.xml`
- Tags are case-sensitive, slash-separated hierarchy (e.g., `Sensory-event/Visual/Color/Red`)
- Library schemas extend standard schema; both can be active simultaneously
- Distinguish HED specification version (3.x.x) from schema version (8.x.x)

## Related resources

- [HED Specification](https://www.hedtags.com/hed-specification)
- [HED Schemas Repository](https://github.com/hed-standard/hed-schemas)
- [HED Online Tools](https://hedtools.org/hed)
